quote:
When a people decide to change their steward, the river of opinion must flow beyond the old banks before the new winds can bend the sails.

A truce is merely the pause of an old drum; without a new rhythm, the song of peace will forever stay unplayed.

When a songbird's last note fades, its silence whispers louder than all the applause it once received.

In a crowd where many steps collapse beneath an unseen weight, the gentlest hand is the one that can lift the invisible load.

A seeker searching through endless scrolls may find the largest page unprinted: the one that holds unseen truths.

An armor forged in fire is only as strong as the trust placed in it; broken faith turns metallic dreams into rust.

When a nation's heart chants against a blade of fear, even the sharpest words may find their echo swallowed by the silent storm.
quote_preview: When a people decide to change their steward, the river of opinion must flow beyond the old banks before the new winds can bend the sails.
